sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a chief complaint of lower back pain radiating down to the left leg, which has been ongoing for a while, with a significant exacerbation over the past month.
The pain is described as an electrical toothache and is worse with standing and walking, but improved with laying down.
The patient rates the pain as a 6 out of 10, with a worst-case scenario of 13 out of 10.
The patient has a history of a fall from a roof 30 years ago, which may have resulted in a possible fracture.
Past Medical History: The patient has a history of back pain, for which they have tried NSAIDs and physical therapy (PT) without relief.
The patient also has a history of a friend's comment about nerve damage, but no further information is provided.
Review of Systems: The patient reports numbness and tingling in the lower extremities, with weakness in both legs, and pain with coughing and sneezing.
Current Medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital Signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical Exam: On examination, the patient has pain to palpation at the L4 vertebrae, and pain with flexion and extension of the back.
The patient has 4 out of 5 strength in the left leg and 5 out of 5 strength in the right leg.
Reflexes are brisk and motor and sensory are intact in both lower extremities.
Test Results: MRI results show a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae with nerve root impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement, causing lower back pain and radiating pain down the left leg.
Problem List: Acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement; lower back pain; radiating pain down the left leg; numbness and tingling in the lower extremities; weakness in both legs.
Plan:
I will recommend an epidural steroid injection to place anti-inflammatory medication at the site of inflammation.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the area.
The patient will follow up with me in about a month to assess the effectiveness of the injection.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in about a month to assess the effectiveness of the injection.
